title: Replace fake Jules validation with a documented live routing contract
objective: Work out the safest live routing contract for Jules so the controller can move beyond fake runtime tests without fragmenting between the generic runtime_commands adapter, Gemini's Jules extension flow, and remote subagent paths.
context: Current ACP coverage proves only a fake Jules runtime wired through runtime_commands.jules.
Gemini CLI docs already describe a Jules extension and remote subagent concepts.
The repo does not yet have one canonical live Jules path for the controller layer.
constraints: Prefer one canonical live route instead of supporting every path equally.
Keep PCC in front of the live route.
Document failure and retry boundaries for unavailable Jules runtimes.
required_evidence: A recommendation for the primary live Jules route.
Tradeoffs vs the other available routes.
A plan for how to validate the live route without destabilizing ACP regressions.
success_criteria: The controller layer has one documented live Jules routing decision.
The design includes validation and fallback behavior.
The route can later replace or complement the fake runtime test.

next_actions.allow: Document the chosen live route and add the first real validation hook.
next_actions.review: Reduce the comparison set and decide between only two routes.
next_actions.reject: Postpone live routing until the controller ledger and report reader exist.
next_actions.retry: Retry once live Jules connectivity is available.
